name: safari.settings
title: Safari:Settings
description: The declaration to configure Safari settings.

payloadkeys.title: Accept Cookies
payloadkeys.content:
The policy Safari uses for managing cookies:

- `Never`: Safari always blocks cookies.
- `CurrentWebsite`: Safari allows cookies only from the current website.
- `VisitedWebsites`: Safari allows cookies only from visited websites.
- `Always`: Safari always allows cookies.

payloadkeys.title: Allow Disabling Fraud Warning
payloadkeys.content: If `false`, the system forces fraud warnings on in Safari.

payloadkeys.title: Allow History Clearing
payloadkeys.content: If `false`, the system disables clearing history in Safari.

payloadkeys.title: Allow JavaScript
payloadkeys.content: If `false`, the system disables JavaScript in Safari.

payloadkeys.title: Allow Private Browsing
payloadkeys.content: If `false`, the system disables private browsing in Safari.

payloadkeys.title: Allow Popups
payloadkeys.content: If `false`, the system disables popups in Safari.

payloadkeys.title: Allow Summary
payloadkeys.content: If `false`, the system disables summarization of content in Safari.

payloadkeys.title: New Tab Start Page
payloadkeys.content: Sets the start page for new tabs in Safari.

subkeys.content:
Sets the start page type in Safari:

- `Start` - Safari uses the default start page. Safari disables the Homepage.
- `Home` - Safari uses the page specified by `HomepageURL`, and Safari also sets that as the Homepage.
- `Extension` - Safari uses the page specified by the Safari extension whose identifier is `ExtensionIdentifier`. Safari disables the Homepage.

subkeys.title: The homepage URL.
subkeys.content: The URL of the homepage which needs to start with `https://` or `http://`. Required when setting `PageType` to `Home`.

subkeys.title: The extension identifier.
subkeys.content: The composed identifier of the extension that provides the start page. The required format is "Identifier (TeamIdentifier)", for example "com.example.app (ABCD1234)". Required when setting `PageType` to `Extension`.
